hero.title: One console for your whole local agent stack
hero.sub: A management console for the Null stack: one binary with a built-in web dashboard and CLI that installs NullClaw agents, NullBoiler workflows, NullTickets task queues and NullWatch tracing, keeps them running, and updates them with rollback. Everything stays on your machine — all state lives in ~/.nullhub, nothing goes to a hosted service.

quickstart:
Start the server, then add components from the same binary — the terminal wizard and the web UI run the same manifest-driven flow.

```bash
# start the server and open the dashboard
nullhub
# → http://nullhub.localhost:19800

# add components (terminal wizard)
nullhub install nullclaw
nullhub install nullwatch

# run and inspect
nullhub start-all
nullhub status
```

features.title: Manifest-driven install wizard
features.body: Add a component through a guided setup flow — in the terminal or in the browser. Each component ships a nullhub-manifest.json that tells NullHub how to install, configure, launch and health-check it, so NullHub stays a generic engine and new components need no changes to it.

features.title: Process supervision
features.body: Start, stop, restart, crash recovery with backoff. Periodic HTTP health checks feed the dashboard status cards.

features.title: Updates with rollback
features.body: check-updates, update, update-all: download the new version, migrate config, roll back on failure.

features.title: Live logs over SSE
features.body: Tail or follow logs per instance: `nullhub logs nullclaw/main -f` in the terminal, live streaming over SSE (server-sent events) in the browser.

features.title: Multi-instance
features.body: Run several instances of the same component side by side. Everything is addressed as component/instance-name.

features.title: Web UI, CLI and raw API
features.body: Browser dashboard for humans, CLI for automation, and nullhub api for raw JSON access to every endpoint.

note: Pre-1.0 and calendar-versioned (v2026.x.y): config and CLI may change between releases.

plain.what: A management console for locally run Null services — a single Zig binary with an embedded web dashboard and a CLI, serving at http://nullhub.localhost:19800.
plain.why: Running NullClaw (agents), NullBoiler (workflows), NullTickets (task queues) and NullWatch (tracing) by hand means four separate daemons to install, configure, restart and update — NullHub replaces that with one supervisor, one dashboard and one CLI.
plain.when: You are setting up the Null stack on a machine and want to add components through a guided wizard, watch their health and logs in a browser, and run `nullhub update-all` instead of updating each daemon by hand.
